topic: In China employee termination: checklist-led payroll execution playbook for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Provides legal framework for termination handling and compensation exposure.
Supports documentation and process consistency expectations in employment disputes.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Supports social insurance obligations relevant to termination-month closure operations.
Anchors offboarding consistency requirements affecting payroll completion.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Provides payroll tax context for final-pay component handling and annual reconciliation environment.
Supports documenting treatment assumptions at component level.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Highlights documentation and process discipline as major risk controls for multinational employers in China.
Supports operations-first approach to termination execution quality.

consensus: Termination compliance outcomes depend heavily on execution control quality.
Checklist-led governance helps foreign employers reduce preventable rework and disputes.
Contested components should be routed through controlled exception handling rather than rushed in-cycle release.
uncertainties: City-level interpretation and evidence expectations can vary by case details.
Some component-treatment details may require local confirmation before release.
English-language source summaries may not capture all Chinese-language implementation nuance.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Use condition-based release checklists (pre-release and post-release) to standardize go/no-go decisions for each termination case.
